local_names.romanized_local: Yáng Táo (Mandarin); Belimbing (Malay/Indonesian); Ma-fuang (Thai); Kamaranga (Tamil); Kamarkha (Hindi)

region_of_origin: Southeast Asia (native); widely cultivated in Malaysia, Thailand, Indonesia, and Singapore
food_subgroup: Fresh tropical fruit — waxy yellow-green edible skin; crispy juicy flesh; sweet-sour flavour; sliced crosswise into distinctive 5-pointed star shapes

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Averrhoa carambola (starfruit). Closest parent is tropical fruit / drupe. No URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Carambola (starfruit) — FoodEx2 code A00LC under the category 'Tropical and subtropical fruits'. Defensible exact match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No confirmed SNOMED CT concept for starfruit as a food item found in publicly available SNOMED CT browser. Null assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium starfruit weighs approximately 120 g and provides about 37 kcal, making it a very low-calorie snack suitable for most dietary patterns.

nutrient_source.title: Carambola (starfruit), raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDCID 167748; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient database
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (SR Legacy) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDCID 167748 is an exact match for raw carambola. Values cross-checked against HPB Singapore Foodo. All per-100g values are consistent between both sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Carambola (Starfruit) — University of Sydney GI Database reference values for Averrhoa carambola
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~35 (low) sourced from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for carambola. Glycemic load per 120g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (35 × 8.04g) / 100 ≈ 2.8. Confidence 0.9 as the University of Sydney database lists a small number of test subjects for this fruit.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (2 mg/100g); good potassium content (133 mg/100g) supporting favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Starfruit is suitable for hypertensive patients with normal kidney function due to its very low sodium and moderate potassium content; however, patients with hypertension-related CKD must strictly avoid starfruit due to caramboxin neurotoxicity risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~35), very low glycemic load per serving (~2.8), very low calorie (37 kcal per 120g fruit)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Starfruit is a genuinely suitable low-GI fruit snack for T2DM patients with normal kidney function; however, since T2DM is the leading cause of CKD in Singapore, clinicians must screen for CKD before advising starfruit consumption, as any degree of CKD is an absolute contraindication due to caramboxin accumulation causing potentially fatal neurotoxicity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, low calorie, good dietary fibre (2.8g/100g), and Vitamin C (34.4 mg/100g) with antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Starfruit is appropriate for cardiovascular disease patients with normal renal function given its negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and antioxidant Vitamin C content; patients with CVD-related or comorbid CKD must avoid starfruit entirely due to caramboxin neurotoxicity.

chronic_kidney_disease.key_factor: Caramboxin — a neurotoxin in starfruit that is normally renally cleared but accumulates fatally in CKD patients of any stage
chronic_kidney_disease.guidance: Starfruit is STRICTLY CONTRAINDICATED in all patients with CKD regardless of cause or stage; caramboxin accumulation causes progressive neurological deterioration including hiccups, vomiting, limb weakness, altered consciousness, seizures, and death, as documented in multiple Singapore and Malaysian case reports.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starfruit (Carambola / 杨桃 / Belimbing), scientific name Averrhoa carambola, is a distinctively shaped tropical fruit native to Southeast Asia and widely sold at Singapore hawker fruit stalls and wet markets year-round. Its 5-ribbed cross-section produces perfect star shapes when sliced, making it visually iconic. The waxy, translucent yellow-green to golden flesh is crispy, juicy, and mildly sweet-sour, commonly served with asam boi (sour plum) dipping powder at Singapore fruit stalls. Nutritionally, starfruit is excellent for most people: very low calorie at 31 kcal/100g (approximately 37 kcal per medium 120g fruit), very low GI (~35, low category), good dietary fibre (2.8g/100g), meaningful Vitamin C (34.4 mg/100g), and negligible sodium (2 mg/100g). It is naturally vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. CRITICAL CLINICAL SAFETY WARNING: Starfruit contains caramboxin, a neurotoxin that is normally cleared by healthy kidneys but accumulates dangerously in patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD) of any cause or stage. CKD patients who consume starfruit can develop progressive neurological deterioration — hiccups, vomiting, limb weakness, altered consciousness, seizures, and potentially death. This syndrome is well-documented in Singapore and Malaysian clinical literature. Since T2DM is the leading cause of CKD in Singapore, a significant proportion of diabetic patients are at risk, making starfruit safety one of the most practically important dietary counselling points for Singapore clinicians managing diabetes and renal disease.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDCID 167748 (exact match for raw carambola), cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo. GI value from University of Sydney GI Database. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully populated from approved primary sources.
notes: Saturated fat is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy (raw fruit with negligible saturated fatty acids). Trans fat is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. Cholesterol is confirmed zero — naturally zero as this is a plant-based fruit. Vitamin B12 is confirmed zero — naturally zero as this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived components. Vitamin D is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for raw carambola. Monounsaturated fat (0.017g) and polyunsaturated fat (0.166g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDCID 167748 detailed fatty acid profile. Water content (91.4g/100g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy. CRITICAL CLINICAL NOTE: Starfruit contains caramboxin, a neurotoxin strictly contraindicated in all CKD patients regardless of stage or cause. FoodEx2 code A00LC assigned for carambola under tropical and subtropical fruits category — defensible exact match. FoodOn URI and SNOMED CT code left null as no confirmed codes exist for starfruit in publicly available ontology browsers; gap notes provided.
